name: Implement .layout-master-detail layout primitive
description:
Add a master-detail layout primitive: list pane + detail pane with responsive behavior.

From docs/COMPONENT_COVERAGE.md, identified as one of 6 real remaining gaps. Also noted in Dex u8o1g3lw (layout primitive grill).

Spec:
- Class in @layer layouts: .layout-master-detail
- Two-column grid: list pane (typically 33% or fixed-width) + detail pane (1fr)
- Mobile collapse: at < 768px, only one pane visible at a time. Either:
  - URL-driven (when path has /id, show detail; root path shows list) — author wires nav
  - :has(.detail.active) on the container to control which pane shows
- Detail pane has its own scroll context (overflow-y: auto; min-block-size: 0)
- List pane scrolls independently (sticky on desktop)
- Back-to-list affordance: built-in or author-provided?

Open implementation questions for the implementer:
- Selector for active state (data-detail-open? :has(.detail:not(:empty))? URL-driven via :target?)
- Whether to ship a 'back to list' button or expect author composition
- Width of list pane: fixed (~320px) or proportional (33%)

Reference: every email client, every settings UI, every chat app with a sidebar of conversations uses this shape. Currently authors compose .layout-sidebar.fill but the responsive behavior is wrong (sidebar takes 50% on mobile per Dex u8o1g3lw bug).

Size: M
Composes with .app-shell for full-height behavior. May share the .layout-sidebar.fill mobile fix from Dex u8o1g3lw.

name: Implement .banner and .empty components (ADR 0004)
description:
Implement the components defined in docs/adr/0004-banner-and-empty-state-components.md.

.banner spec:
- Class in @layer components in src/lib/drop-in.css
- display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--vs-m); padding: var(--pad-m) var(--pad-l)
- Three logical slots in source order: icon (optional, sized to ~1.5em), text content (flex: 1; can contain headline + body), actions (margin-inline-start: auto)
- Full-width by default (no max-inline-size)
- Border-block: 1px solid var(--callout-border-color); no border-radius (edge-to-edge feel)
- Variants reuse --callout-color / --callout-border-color: .warning, .error, .success, .ghost
- .banner.sticky modifier: position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10 (or appropriate from system)
- NO dismiss button styling, NO popover plumbing — author writes whatever close mechanism they want

.empty spec:
- Class in @layer components
- display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; text-align: center
- gap: var(--vs-m); padding: var(--empty-pad, var(--pad-xxxl))
- max-inline-size: 48ch on text children (or set max-inline-size on .empty itself and let children naturally wrap)
- Children in source order: icon (sized via container constraint), headline (consider --fl: 2), body (color: var(--fg-6)), actions
- Inside .card or .surface, --empty-pad should reduce (consumer override or :where(.card, .surface) > .empty selector)

Tasks:
1. Add .banner and .empty rules to @layer components in src/lib/drop-in.css
2. Run npm run build to regenerate raw.js
3. Add demo markup to index.html showing both components and color variants
4. Update skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECIPES_COMPONENTS.md
5. Verify no clash with existing .empty / .banner names (grep first)
6. Consider whether .empty needs a .full modifier for route-level empties with min-block-size

Open question for implementer: should .banner.sticky use top: 0 directly, or expose --banner-sticky-offset for apps that have a fixed nav above? Defaulting to 0 with var() override is the safe path.

name: Aesthetic presets: framework prep
description:
Declare the new `@layer themes` and expand the font roster so the v1 preset catalog is buildable.

## Why
Per ADR-0003 (docs/adr/0003-aesthetic-preset-architecture.md), aesthetic presets live in a new `@layer themes` slotted between `base` and `components` in the cascade. The layer must be declared in core `drop-in.css` so the order is stable even when no preset import is present. Two of the v1 presets (`theme-editorial`, `theme-paper`) are undeliverable until the `ThemeControls.svelte` font roster gains at least one serif and one display-condensed face — the current 12 options are all sans or mono.

## Scope
- Edit `src/lib/drop-in.css` cascade-layer declaration
- Edit `src/docs/ThemeControls.svelte` `fontOptions` array
- Verify ADR-0002 cascade contract still holds (layouts highest, utilities above components)

## Done when
- `@layer base, themes, components, utilities, layouts;` declared in drop-in.css
- At least one variable serif and one display-condensed font added to fontOptions
- Existing demos render unchanged (no visual regressions on /elements, /ui-blocks, /templates/*)

name: Fix docs topic hierarchy readability
description:
## What
Adjust TopicSection rendering so long markdown-heavy routes (especially /elements and /ui-blocks) remain visually readable after single-source migration.

## Why
Topic sections feel visually overwhelming because markdown subsection headings render too prominently relative to topic headings.

## Scope
- Update src/docs/TopicSection.svelte only.
- Keep demos and anchors intact.
- Improve topic section readability by preserving readable width and demoting markdown heading levels at render-time without mutating source markdown files.

## Verification
- Use agent-browser on http://localhost:6124 to verify /elements and /ui-blocks visually.
- Confirm heading hierarchy is topic heading > markdown subheading in rendered page.
- Capture screenshots for both routes.
result: Updated src/docs/TopicSection.svelte to improve docs readability: topic sections now use readable width + demo spacing (readable demo-section), and runtime heading normalization demotes markdown heading levels (outside fenced code blocks) so subsection headers are visually subordinate to topic titles. Verified with agent-browser on http://localhost:6124 for /elements and /ui-blocks, including refreshed visual snapshots (/tmp/elements-after-hierarchy-fix-v2.png, /tmp/ui-blocks-after-hierarchy-fix-v2.png) and unchanged demo counts (elements=22, ui-blocks=17). Ran npm run check:docs-content and npm run build (pass with existing known href '#' a11y warnings).

name: Stat card component (.stat-card)
description: Dashboard template hand-builds KPI/stat cards with label, big number, and change tag. Common dashboard pattern. Add a dedicated .stat-card component with proper structure and visual polish.
result: Closed as stale (~76 days untouched). Not pursued.

name: Add mobile app shell layout (iOS-safe)
description:
Create a mobile-first app shell layout that works perfectly on iOS Safari/Chrome. Requirements:
- Respect safe-area insets (top/bottom) for notch/home indicator
- Avoid 100vh issues (use dvh/svh or CSS-only strategy)
- Sticky top/bottom bars without content jump
- Main content scrolls (no double scroll)
- Works when URL bar hides/shows
- Compatible with light/dark themes
- Provide a demo and docs example
Acceptance: no layout glitches on modern iOS Safari.
result: Added .app-shell layout with grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto, safe area variables (--safe-top/bottom/left/right using env()), sticky header/footer. Uses 100dvh for iOS URL bar issues. Added demo in Mobile.svelte.

name: 7. Create dynamic route page for /base
description:
## What Replace src/routes/(docs)/base/+page.svelte with a dynamic page that renders topics from .md files. ## Why This is the first route migration. We're doing one route at a time to catch issues early. ## Implementation ### Step 1: Save baseline ```bash curl -s -H 'Accept: text/markdown' http://localhost:6124/base > /tmp/base-agent-before.md ``` ### Step 2: Create new page Replace src/routes/(docs)/base/+page.svelte: ```svelte

Base

Foundation styles and variables.

There is a deep base of classless CSS defaults in Graffiti giving you good defaults without having to memorize a bunch of classes...

{#each topics as topic (topic.id)} {/each} ``` ### Step 3: Verify agent markdown unchanged ```bash curl -s -H 'Accept: text/markdown' http://localhost:6124/base > /tmp/base-agent-after.md diff /tmp/base-agent-before.md /tmp/base-agent-after.md ``` ### Step 4: Visual verification - Open http://localhost:6124/base in browser - Compare visually to current site - Check: All headings present? All demos render? Code highlighting works? ## Current Sections in base/+page.svelte 1. Typography (h1-h6, paragraph) 2. Variables & Tokens (spacing, radius, border, padding, line-height, shadows) 3. Colors (color scales, theming variables) 4. Dividers (hr) 5. Forms (text inputs, checkboxes, radios, date/time, range, file, validation) Each section should map to a topic in src/docs/content/topics/base-*.md ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All sections render - [ ] All demos show live preview + code - [ ] Deep links work (#typography, #variables, #colors, #forms) - [ ] Agent markdown identical before/after - [ ] pnpm build passes ## Files - REPLACE: src/routes/(docs)/base/+page.svelte
result: Replaced src/routes/(docs)/base/+page.svelte with runtime-driven rendering using getRouteIntro('base') and getTopicsForRoute('base'), then rendered each topic through TopicSection. Verified agent markdown parity with before/after curl + diff (no differences), ran npm run check:docs-content and npm run build, and validated in agent-browser on /base (topic anchors present, demos render, no error overlay).

name: Phase 3.2: Add CONTEXT.md Template glossary entry
description:
## Goal
Add a Template entry to CONTEXT.md so the glossary covers the now-load-bearing concept.

## Where
CONTEXT.md, in the Language section, alphabetical position.

## Entry text (use as-is or tighten wording)
**Template**: A finished, theme-agnostic page exemplar at src/routes/templates/<slug>. Demonstrates a coherent product surface (auth flow, pricing, dashboard, blog) built entirely from Graffiti primitives, with no inline aesthetic overrides. Templates render polished in Graffiti's default appearance and degrade gracefully under aesthetic presets (ADR 0003). The canonical example surface consumed by AI agents via the skill and the /templates Accept: text/markdown route.
_Avoid_: 'page', 'example', 'demo' - these don't carry the theme-agnostic / no-inline-aesthetic-overrides contract.

## Relationships section update
Add: A **template** consumes **components**, **layout primitives**, and **utilities** but never carries an **aesthetic preset** in its source - the preset is the consumer's choice at :root.

## Out of scope
Do NOT add 'Template highlight' as a glossary term - the manifest schema speaks for itself in code. Glossary stays at concept level.

## Done when
- CONTEXT.md has the Template entry with Avoid list
- Relationships section updated
- Entry cross-references ADR 0006 and ADR 0003

name: Author skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/AESTHETIC_PRESETS.md
description: Create the new reference file. One section per v1 preset (editorial, brutalist, soft-consumer, neon-arcade, paper). Each section: (1) aesthetic territory in one paragraph; (2) intended use cases; (3) when NOT to use (anti-patterns); (4) how to apply — html-level vs container-scoped — with code snippets; (5) what token overrides pair well on top; (6) what to avoid combining (e.g. dont mix theme-brutalist with the soft-consumer color theme — they fight). Link to ADR-0003 for the architecture rationale and to CONTEXT.md glossary for the Aesthetic preset definition.

name: Implement .fab (floating action button)
description:
Add a floating action button (FAB) component for mobile/single-primary-action patterns.

From docs/COMPONENT_COVERAGE.md, identified as one of 6 real remaining gaps.

Spec:
- Class in @layer components: .fab (or .button.fab modifier — implementer choice)
- Position: fixed; bottom + right (or insets via env(safe-area-inset-*) for iOS notch)
- Size: ~56px diameter (Material precedent) but token-driven via --fab-size
- Border-radius: 50% by default; pill shape with text via .fab.extended
- Background: var(--primary); color: auto-contrast or var(--bg)
- Box-shadow elevated (uses existing --s-* tokens)
- Z-index above app-shell content but below dialogs
- Touch target ≥ 48px regardless of size
- Reduced motion: incidental transitions stop, no required --animation-reduced variant unless an entrance animation is added
- Variants: .fab.extended (with label text), positioned modifiers .fab.start (bottom-left) for RTL contexts

Size: S
Markup:
<button class="fab" aria-label="New message">
  <svg>...</svg>
</button>

Composition: in app-shell, sits outside <main> as a sibling of the layout content so it floats above scroll

name: Author src/lib/themes/soft-consumer.css (.theme-soft-consumer)
description: Token overrides: humanist sans --font-sans (e.g. DM Sans or Plus Jakarta), generous --br-* (xs=6px through xxl=64px), heavy --shadow-* presence, friendly palette (saturated --primary, warm --fg/--bg balance). Selector rules: minimal — possibly soft .theme-soft-consumer ::selection. Token-dominant per ADR-0003.

name: Update OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md for aesthetic-intent case
description: Edit skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md. Add: when the user prompt expresses an aesthetic intent, the output must include a Selected aesthetic preset and rationale section identifying which v1 preset was chosen and why. If no aesthetic intent was expressed, no preset is applied — omit the section. New failure mode to enumerate: applying a preset when none was requested (over-application is as bad as under-application).

result: Added .text-muted (--fg-5) and .text-faint (--fg-3). Replaced 30+ inline color styles across all 4 templates.

name: Add <figure> and <figcaption> element-default styling
description:
Add general-purpose <figure>/<figcaption> styling in @layer base.

From docs/COMPONENT_COVERAGE.md, identified as one of 6 real remaining gaps. Currently only sized inside .card (line 1999 in drop-in.css).

Spec:
- In @layer base in src/lib/drop-in.css
- figure: margin-block: var(--vs-l); margin-inline: 0; max-inline-size: 100%
- figure > :is(img, picture, video) { inline-size: 100%; block-size: auto; border-radius: var(--br-m) }
- figcaption: margin-block-start: var(--vs-s); font-size: 0.875em; color: var(--fg-6); text-align: center (or start, decide)
- Within .layout-readable, figure can break out via .full-bleed modifier (already exists)
- Within .card, the existing scoping at line 1999 takes over (no change needed)

Considerations:
- Text-align center vs. start for figcaption: most prose conventions center; tech docs prefer start. Decide and document.
- Should figure be a block-level layout primitive or stay as element-default? Stays as element-default per base-layer scope.

Size: S
This is genuine base-layer element styling, not a new component.

name: Phase 2.13: Execute visual QA matrix and final verification

name: Phase 2: Improve Graffiti templates and library ergonomics from skill findings
description:
## Goal
Fix the template and library shortcomings surfaced in Phase 1 so Graffiti is easier for both humans and AI to use correctly.

## Why
Current templates demonstrate capability but contain significant inline-style debt and inconsistent composition patterns, which weakens AI reliability and user copy-paste outcomes.

## Scope
- Reduce template inline style debt with reusable class-based primitives
- Add missing utilities and variants in drop-in.css where recurring gaps exist
- Restore and harden AI chat template layout behavior
- Align llms docs with canonical template usage
- Add quality guardrails to prevent regressions
- Audit every template and key feature cluster at mobile breakpoints

## Deliverables
- Refactored templates in src/routes/templates/
- Library improvements in src/lib/drop-in.css (and related docs/demo updates)
- Updated llms guidance reflecting canonical patterns
- Repeatable checks for inline style budget and template quality
- Mobile breakpoint audit evidence in phase reports and task results

## Done When
- Templates are materially cleaner and more class-first
- Missing primitives are implemented where repeated inline workarounds existed
- AI chat template is no longer hidden by quality concerns
- Visual and build verification pass across target templates
- Mobile breakpoint audits pass for each template and core feature group
result: Historical record of templates cleanup attempt (2026-03-12 to 2026-05-17). 9 of 13 children completed: foundational utilities (2.2), component variants (2.3), app-shell/sidebar hardening (2.4), template index refactor (2.5), and retrofits of landing (2.6), dashboard (2.7), blog (2.8), settings (2.9), plus baseline (2.1). 4 in-flight children closed as superseded by Phase 3, which codifies the implicit rule into ADR 0006, builds new templates (auth, pricing, PDP, profile, search, inbox stretch), and re-audits the four completed retrofits under the codified rule.

name: Add curated gradient utility classes

Each gradient is a fully designed composition using light-dark() - specific gradient type (linear/radial/conic), angle/position, color stops all baked in. Single class = beautiful result. Includes .gradient-text modifier. Gradients: sunset, ocean, aurora, midnight, dawn, forest, lavender, neon, slate, surface. Lives in @layer utilities in drop-in.css. Add demo in src/docs/demos/Gradients.svelte and section in utilities docs page.

name: Implement atomic primitives: .badge/.dot, <kbd>, code blocks
description:
Three small primitives from grilling session 2026-05-15. .badge and .dot (component, @layer components in src/lib/drop-in.css): - Mechanism: CSS anchor positioning, matching the existing tooltip + dropdown-menu patterns - Canonical markup: 3 - .badge styling: position: absolute; top: anchor(top); right: anchor(right); transform: translate(33%, -33%); padding: 0 0.4em; min-inline-size: 1.25em; block-size: 1.25em; border-radius: var(--br-pill); background: var(--error); color: var(--bg); font-size: 0.75em; line-height: 1.25em; text-align: center - .dot variant: same positioning, no content. Smaller (block-size: 0.625em; min-inline-size: 0.625em). Default color: var(--green) (online indicator). Author overrides via inline style or composition. - Color modifiers: .badge.error (default), .badge.success (--green), .badge.muted (--fg-3), .badge.primary (--primary) - Position modifiers: .badge.bottom-right, .badge.top-left, .badge.bottom-left (default top-right) - Browser support: anchor positioning is Chrome 125+ / Safari 18+ / Firefox flag. Document the requirement; for older browsers, authors can opt-in to absolute positioning fallback (.badge.absolute requires position: relative on host). in @layer base: - Style element directly: display: inline-block; padding: 0.15em 0.4em; font-family: var(--font-mono, ui-monospace, monospace); font-size: 0.875em; line-height: 1; border: 1px solid var(--fg-2); border-block-end-width: 2px; border-radius: var(--br-s); background: var(--fg-1); color: var(--fg-7); vertical-align: middle; min-inline-size: 1.5em; text-align: center - The thicker bottom border gives a subtle 'key cap' tactile feel 
 and  in @layer base:
- Inline : font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.875em; padding: 0.1em 0.3em; background: var(--fg-1); border-radius: var(--br-s); color: var(--fg-7)
- Block 
: font-family: var(--font-mono); padding: var(--pad-l); background: var(--surface-bg, var(--fg-1)); border-radius: var(--br-m); overflow-x: auto; line-height: 1.5; tab-size: 2; color: var(--fg-7)
- Block 
: reset inline padding/background to inherit from 
; just font-family
- Syntax highlighting is explicitly OUT of scope (needs JS / a library). Authors who want highlighting bring their own (Shiki, Prism, hljs).

Tasks:
1. Add all rules to correct layers in src/lib/drop-in.css
2. Run npm run build to regenerate raw.js
3. Add demo markup to index.html: bell icon with .badge, avatar with .dot online indicator, paragraph with ⌘+K, inline  in prose, fenced 
 block
4. Update skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECIPES_COMPONENTS.md
5. Document anchor-positioning browser-support note in skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/GRAFFITI_SYSTEM.md (this is the third use of anchor positioning after tooltip and dropdown-menu; the system-level commitment is now visible)

name: Implement .hover-card component
description:
Add a hover-card component: like a tooltip but interactive (can contain links, persists while hovered). From docs/COMPONENT_COVERAGE.md, identified as one of 6 real remaining gaps after the 2026-05-15 grilling session. Spec: - Class in @layer components in src/lib/drop-in.css - Mechanism: CSS anchor positioning (extends the pattern already used by .tooltip, .dropdown-menu, .badge) - Markup pattern: @scott
…rich content with links, avatar, button…
- Trigger: hover or focus on .hover-card-trigger shows the .hover-card. Use [popover=hint] for light-dismiss semantics + CSS :hover/:focus-within on the trigger element to invoke .showPopover() — note: invoking popover from CSS hover requires the showPopover() JS call. Pure HTML+CSS shows via :popover-open via :hover on a wrapping label, OR uses anchor-positioning + opacity transition with :has(.hover-card-trigger:hover) on a parent. - Pure-CSS path (no JS): wrapper with .hover-card-host { position: relative; .hover-card { display: none } &:hover .hover-card, &:focus-within .hover-card { display: block } } — anchor positioning still works. - @starting-style for fade-in entry - transition-behavior: allow-discrete for display none<->block transitions - --animation-reduced contract per ADR 0001 if any transitions added Size: M Differentiator from .tooltip: tooltip is non-interactive text-only with :hover delay; .hover-card persists while user moves into it, contains interactive children

name: Aesthetic presets: demo proof — canonical UI under each preset
description:
Build a side-by-side demo that proves each preset really transforms the look by rendering the same canonical UI under every preset.

The grilling that produced ADR-0003 surfaced that the existing 14 color themes in ThemeControls.svelte "look the same" because they are orthogonal axis selections, not coordinated aesthetic presets. The v1 preset claim — five distinct, opinionated, recognisable looks — has to be visually demonstrable, not just asserted.

Canonical fixture (rendered identically under every preset): buttons (primary/secondary/ghost/disabled), a .card with title+body+action, a form (label+input+select+checkbox row + .form-actions), a native <dialog> in the open state, a blog excerpt (h1, h2, paragraph, blockquote), and a tag/chip cluster.

name: Author src/lib/themes/paper.css (.theme-paper)
description: Token overrides: serif --font-sans, off-white --bg (#fafaf5 or similar), ink-black --fg, narrow --lh-* values, --br-* near 0 (xs=0, xxl<=4px), --shadow-* none. Inside @layer themes: optionally subtle .theme-paper ::selection in ink color. Distinct from editorial: editorial is web-feel for long-form, paper is print-feel — narrower line height, smaller ratio, no drop cap. Token-dominant per ADR-0003.

name: Strengthen graffiti-best-practices guardrails
description: Follow-up hardening requested: make Graffiti baseline/system identity explicit upfront, add mandatory system-first preflight, require primitive mapping for each requested component before writing markup, add hard-fail rules against re-implementing built-ins (dialog/modal, card, bubble/chat, callout, form primitives), add required post-edit compliance report proving no duplicate styling system, and include canonical critical snippets (including native dialog + .close pattern).
result: Strengthened graffiti-best-practices with explicit Graffiti-baseline identity, mandatory system-first preflight (variables/theme/layout/utilities/components), primitive mapping requirement, and hard-fail rules for re-implementing built-ins or creating duplicate styling systems. Added canonical critical snippets (including native dialog + .close), upgraded output contract with preflight/mapping/compliance report sections, and updated anti-pattern/troubleshooting/component recipe guidance to enforce canonical primitive reuse.

name: Phase 3.4: Update renderTemplateMarkdown for structured highlights + drift check
description:
## Goal
Two changes to src/docs/agent/markdown.js, both flowing from the new manifest schema (Phase 3.3):

1. **Emit per-pattern metadata in template MD.** Today renderTemplateMarkdown emits 'Common Classes' as a flat list at the template level. With structured highlights, emit per-pattern subsections with: name (as heading), classes, tokens, anchor link to the rendered page (#anchor), gapNotes when present (as 'Known limitations').

2. **Manifest-source drift check at render time.** Mirror the pattern in getOrderedRuntimeTopics (lines 113-156): assert that for every highlight, each listed class actually appears in the corresponding .svelte source, and the anchor (when present) matches a <section id=...> in source. Throw with a clear error pointing to the divergence. Same fail-loud philosophy as the existing topic-manifest validation.

## Files
- src/docs/agent/markdown.js (renderTemplateMarkdown, renderTemplatesIndexMarkdown)
- Possibly a new helper for source-reading: src/docs/agent/templateSource.js

## Acceptance
- Fetching /templates/landing with Accept: text/markdown returns per-pattern subsections with classes / tokens / gapNotes (where present)
- Renderer throws at build time when manifest references classes not present in source OR anchors not present in source
- Existing test/build passes for all retrofitted manifest entries from Phase 3.3

## Reference
- ADR 0006
- src/docs/agent/markdown.js getOrderedRuntimeTopics for the throw-on-mismatch pattern

name: Phase 3.1: Draft ADR 0006 - theme-agnostic templates
description:
## Goal
Pin the theme-agnostic-templates rule in an ADR so the rest of Phase 3 can cite it. Without this, the rule lives only in conversation and the skill text and is vulnerable to drift.

## Where
docs/adr/0006-theme-agnostic-templates.md

## ADR must state
1. **Templates are theme-agnostic exemplars.** They ship un-themed; canonical appearance is Graffiti's defaults. Aesthetic personality (color-scheme, --primary, font, radius, motion) layers on independently via preset classes (ADR 0003) or root-level token overrides applied by the consumer, not via inline overrides in the template source.
2. **Inline-override classification table:**
   - Aesthetic (--primary, --accent, color-scheme, font-family, radius overrides): BANNED at element level. Blocks ship. Fix Graffiti's default or accept that the personality isn't the template's job.
   - Structural-recurring (same composition across templates, e.g., a 'most popular' badge fake): gapNote on the manifest highlight; ship; promote to component/modifier at >=3 instances across the manifest.
   - Structural-one-off (--gap on a specific stack, --narrow-width on a specific block): allowed inline, no note.
3. **Defaults philosophy: neutral-and-clean.** Graffiti's default appearance is deliberately personality-free. Strong-distinctive defaults would make presets fight the baseline, breaking the theme-agnostic property.
4. **Block-ship protocol.** When a template hits an aesthetic gap, the Graffiti fix lands first (its own Dex task; template is blocked-by it). Template lands clean - no workaround in source.
5. **Relationship to ADR 0003 (aesthetic preset architecture).** Presets are an independent axis layered over theme-agnostic templates, NOT a substitute for them. Cross-reference both ways: ADR 0003 should be edited to point back to 0006.

## Considered options to record
- (A) preset-coupled templates (each template names its preset): rejected, would make templates secretly themed and degrade worse under explicit presets
- (B) preset-neutral templates with workarounds allowed: rejected, repeats the Phase 2 failure mode
- (C) theme-agnostic with hard inline-override rule: chosen
- Strong-distinctive defaults: rejected as incoherent with theme-agnostic

## Style
Match existing ADRs 0001-0005 format. Lead with the rule; then Considered options; then Consequences.

## Done when
- File exists at docs/adr/0006-theme-agnostic-templates.md
- ADR 0003 updated with cross-reference back to 0006
- Skill (SKILL.md) and CONTEXT.md updates in subsequent tasks can cite this ADR

result: Refined src/lib/drop-in.css tag styling to reduce gloss and improve readability: lowered gradient/lightness spread and alpha in --tag-bg-top/--tag-bg-bottom, softened border and shadow intensity, weakened inset highlight, and shifted --tag-text to a higher-contrast fixed-lightness tint in both light/dark modes. Also tuned hover/active gradients and highlights to remain subtle. Update skills/graffiti-best-practices so the AI knows aesthetic presets exist, applies them on user intent, and treats preset application as the first move for any theming/aesthetic request.

The /grill-with-docs session that produced ADR-0003 surfaced the central failure mode: the skill optimises for class hygiene and compliance, not visual quality. Related memory: feedback-system-integrity-over-expressiveness (token-dominant authoring is a manifestation of this principle).

name: Re-categorize docs into base, element, forms, ui-components, blocks
description:
Beads description:
Reorganize documentation categories to match the component taxonomy.

FINAL CATEGORIES:

1) BASE - tokens + default element styles (no classes, just foundation)
   - CSS custom property tokens (spacing, colors, shadows, borders, line-heights, easing)
   - Default HTML element styles (h1-h6 elements, body, links, img, etc.)

2) LAYOUT - structural patterns
   - .layout-card, .layout-sidebar, .layout-split, .layout-three-col
   - .layout-readable, .layout-holy-grail, .full-bleed
   - .stack, .cluster, .carousel, .reel, .flex, .grid, .split, .readable

3) ELEMENT - standalone building blocks
   - .box (+ .box-ghost, .box-glow, .box-semi-gloss)
   - button/.button (+ .mini, .primary, .error, .warning, .success, .ghost)
   - table/.table
   - .callout (+ .warning, .error, .ghost, .success, .hard)
   - .circle

4) FORMS - form inputs and validation
   - input, select, textarea base styles
   - label
   - Validation states (.error, .success, .warning)

5) UI-COMPONENTS - composed components
   - .swipe (+ .stop, .full-bleed)

6) BLOCKS - full patterns
   - .header (+ .border, .sticky)

7) UTILITIES - modifier/helper classes
   - .auto-color (+ .chroma)
   - .fc (fluid container)
   - .h1-.h6 (heading styles on non-headings)
   - .fs-* (font size utilities)
   - .visually-hidden, .no-list, .row, .aspect-*

name: 3. Create route intro metadata files
description:
## What
Create src/docs/content/routes/*.md files with intro content for each route.

## Why
Each route page (base, utilities, elements, ui-blocks) has intro prose before the topics. This content needs a home in the single-source system.

## Implementation
Create 4 files:

### src/docs/content/routes/base.md
```markdown
---
title: Base
tagline: Foundation styles and variables.
---

There is a deep base of classless CSS defaults in Graffiti giving you good defaults without having to memorize a bunch of classes. There is also an extensive CSS variable base that is focused on providing a deep system for consistency but also easy customization and theming that works in light or dark mode out of the box.
```

### src/docs/content/routes/utilities.md
```markdown
---
title: CSS Utilities
tagline: These utility classes actually have utility.
---

We aren't replicating CSS with classnames, but providing real functionality to be combined or used individually. If you want a common layout or pattern or even automatic color contrast, we've got you.
```

### src/docs/content/routes/elements.md
```markdown
---
title: Elements
tagline: Single-purpose UI elements.
---

These are common, non-complex UI elements that just need a class or two. Forms, cards, buttons, and more.
```

### src/docs/content/routes/ui-blocks.md
```markdown
---
title: UI Blocks
tagline: Composed multi-element patterns.
---

Larger composed patterns built from Graffiti primitives. Navigation, dialogs, forms, and app shells.
```

## Also
Create src/docs/content/routes/schema.js to validate route metadata.

## Verification
- All 4 route files parse correctly
- Title and tagline accessible at runtime

## Files
- CREATE: src/docs/content/routes/base.md
- CREATE: src/docs/content/routes/utilities.md
- CREATE: src/docs/content/routes/elements.md
- CREATE: src/docs/content/routes/ui-blocks.md
- CREATE: src/docs/content/routes/schema.js (optional)

Added tests/contracts/route-intros.test.ts to ensure runtime access for all configured route intros and undefined for changelog. name: Grill: Meta-system gaps
description:
Stress-test what's missing in how Graffiti positions and sustains itself as a system.

Open threads:
- No canonical 'is this Graffiti-shaped or do I write custom CSS?' rubric. The agent skill has one informally, but it's not in CONTEXT.md or docs. Worth promoting?
- Reduced-motion tax (ADR 0001): every new animated component needs a deliberate --animation-reduced value or an intentional decision to fully stop. Easy to forget. Should there be a test or a lint that flags components in @layer components with animation/transition rules but no reduced-motion contract?
- No contract tests for tokens or cascade layers. If someone reorders @layer or removes a literal token, nothing breaks until a consumer hits it. Worth a vitest-contracts pass that asserts the public token surface?
- No 'extending Graffiti' guide. Consumers customize via CSS var overrides at :root or on a container — but the boundary between 'override a token' and 'write a custom component' isn't documented. Where does customization end and forking begin?
- No versioning / deprecation story I can find. When a class moves layers (like the recent layouts inversion in ADR 0002), how do consumers know? Changeset is wired, but is there a 'breaking change to public surface' definition?
- AGENTS.md is project-internal — there's no equivalent 'how to USE Graffiti' top-level doc beyond README. Is that a gap?
- AGENTS.md doc drift surfaced during 2026-05-15 grill: documents 'dex create -d "Title" --context "Full context..."' but the actual CLI uses positional/-n for name and -d for description (no --context flag). Worth a sweep of AGENTS.md for other CLI drift.
- Naming polysemy: .timeline is being used for both chronological event lists AND ordered process steps (i.e. it's also the stepper). Same shape, two semantic uses. Discoverability question — should there be aliases (.steps), or doc cross-references, or rename, or leave as-is?
- Anchor-positioning commitment: as of 2026-05-15, three components depend on CSS anchor positioning (tooltip, dropdown-menu, badge). Worth formalizing the browser-support floor and adding a system-level note in GRAFFITI_SYSTEM.md / CONTEXT.md.

Resume by running: /grill-with-docs on Graffiti meta-system gaps
result:
Resolved all 9 threads.

ADRs written:
- ADR-0011: Public surface definition + per-ADR deprecation policy (clean removal / one-major deprecation / coexistence)
- ADR-0012: Feature-pinned browser support (Web Platform features are the contract; versions are reference; no polyfills)
- ADR-0013: Contract test enforcement (extends tests/contracts/ to cover ADR-0011 surface; strict --animation-reduced rule for ADR-0001)

Docs written:
- docs/PRINCIPLES.md: canonical six-rule rubric + override-vs-fork boundary (closes T1 and T4)
- docs/BROWSER-SUPPORT.md: required features + reference version table (paired with ADR-0012)

Resolved without artifacts:
- T6 (USE-Graffiti doc gap): no gap; README → docs site → PRINCIPLES.md → CONTEXT.md → skill is sufficient
- T7 (AGENTS.md CLI drift): manual sweep, no automation
- T8 (.timeline polysemy): ship .steps as CSS selector-grouped alias (.timeline, .steps { ... })

name: Phase 3.3: Migrate manifest schema to structured highlights
description:
## Goal
Change the template manifest's highlights field from prose strings to structured objects so AI agents reading template MD get pattern-level metadata (class lists, token overrides, anchors, portability hint, gap notes) instead of just summaries.

## File
src/docs/agent/manifest.js (templatePages array)

## Schema change
Today:
```js
highlights: ['Three-tier pricing with featured plan', 'Six-feature responsive grid', ...]
```

Tomorrow:
```js
highlights: [
  {
    name: 'Three-tier pricing with featured plan',
    classes: ['.pricing-card', '.featured', '.feature-list'],
    tokens: ['--card-bg', '--accent'],
    anchor: 'pricing',          // section id in the .svelte
    portable: true,             // hint: this slice can be lifted out of the template
    gapNotes: null              // or a string when applicable, e.g. 'Currently fakes most-popular badge with .tag - could be .featured-badge modifier'
  },
  ...
]
```

Field semantics:
- name: human-readable, the same value as today's prose string
- classes: every Graffiti class used in this pattern's markup; AI extracts these directly
- tokens: variables overridden at root or in the pattern's own scope (NOT inline aesthetic overrides per ADR 0006)
- anchor: must match a <section id="..."> in the .svelte source (Phase 3.4 adds drift-check)
- portable: true if the pattern can be lifted out of the template into another context unchanged
- gapNotes: string describing structural-recurring friction; null when none; promotes to component/modifier at >=3 across manifest

## Migration work
- Retrofit ALL existing template entries (landing, dashboard, blog, settings, docs-portal, ai-chat) to the structured shape. This is data-only - no template source changes in this task.
- For initial migration, use null for gapNotes and best-guess for portable. Section anchors get added to template source in subsequent retrofit tasks (3.12-3.14).

## Done when
- manifest.js highlights field is structured for every existing template
- TypeScript JSDoc TemplatePage typedef in markdown.js updated to match
- No runtime break (renderTemplateMarkdown still works; emission improvements come in 3.4)

## References
- ADR 0006 (the rule the schema encodes)
- src/docs/agent/markdown.js (typedef at top)

name: Aesthetic presets: ThemeControls integration
description:
Add aesthetic presets to ThemeControls.svelte as a higher-level selector above the existing color / type-scale / radius / font-family axes.

ThemeControls.svelte is the framework existing customization surface — it exposes four orthogonal axes today. Per ADR-0003, aesthetic presets sit above those axes as a coordinated, non-orthogonal artifact.

CONTEXT.md flags an unresolved UX question: when a preset is selected, do the axis dropdowns lock, layer under the preset (axes can override), or hydrate with the preset values for further tweaking? Resolving this is required before implementing 4.2.

Recommended: hydrate then layer — makes preset choices inspectable, lets the user tweak without breaking out of the preset, and the existing Custom option already handles the "I want full control" case.

name: Grill: Token / theming system gaps
description:
Stress-test what is missing in Graffiti's token/theming model.

## Already shipped (out of scope for this grill)
- ADR-0003 — docs/adr/0003-aesthetic-preset-architecture.md — aesthetic presets as opt-in CSS classes in @layer themes, between base and components. Consumes the token system; does not redesign it. v1 catalog: theme-editorial, theme-brutalist, theme-soft-consumer, theme-neon-arcade, theme-paper.
- Work stubbed under 5 epics (none started yet): 0eapo752 (framework prep), qil5yagt (CSS authoring), 5cfksntk (demo proof), g2hvessd (ThemeControls integration), asjjfilp (skill update).
- CONTEXT.md glossary additions: Theme axis (orthogonal override dimension), Aesthetic preset (coordinated, non-orthogonal stack coupling multiple axes).
- Memory: feedback-system-integrity-over-expressiveness — Graffiti extension surfaces must protect structural primitives and component contracts; token-dominant authoring is the canonical path; tokens are THE path, selector rules are an escape hatch.

## Open threads to grill
1. Alpha scale vs. a future lightness scale (flagged in CONTEXT.md). Parallel non-alpha scale via color-mix() with bg/fg for opaque tints? Naming convention to avoid the Tailwind/Radix mental-model clash?
2. Derived-scale cascading hole (LESSONS.md). --primary-1..9, --fg-1..9, --error-1..9 are computed at :root, so overriding the base on a child does NOT recompute the scale. @property registrations to re-resolve on the cascade? Or accept and document the limitation? **Directly limits how powerful the aesthetic presets shipped via ADR-0003 can be when applied to a container rather than :root** — high-leverage thread.
3. Theme-switching story — PARTIALLY ADDRESSED by ADR-0003 presets. Still open: brand-color seed → derived-palette helper? How presets should compose with a brand-color seed override? What is missing between override --primary and full custom theme that presets do not already provide?
4. Auto-color — currently lightness-only on --bg-color. Should it also derive border/muted scales for colored surfaces, or stay narrow?
5. Motion / easing / z-index — systematized into token scales or ad-hoc per component? ADR-0003 deferred motion presets from v1 pending this thread.

## Cross-reference at grill time
- src/lib/drop-in.css — current token surface (~431 vars)
- LESSONS.md — color-scheme + light-dark() footguns; derived-scale gotchas
- src/docs/ThemeControls.svelte — consumer-facing override surface
- docs/adr/0002-cascade-layer-order.md — cascade rationale
- docs/adr/0003-aesthetic-preset-architecture.md — what was just shipped

Resume by running /grill-with-docs with the prompt prepared in the session that created this update.
result:
Resolved all 5 open threads. Three new ADRs written:
- ADR-0006: .theme-scope re-derivation block closes derived-scale cascading hole
- ADR-0007: parallel --{base}-opaque-1..9 symmetric scale (1=tint, 5=base, 9=shade), wide scope
- ADR-0008: --d-* duration + --z-* z-index tier scales; motion now first-class theme axis

Intentional non-features: brand-seed palette derivation (T3), .auto-color expansion (T4), shade-only scale (T1 sub).

CONTEXT.md updated: Theme scope and Opaque scale glossary entries; Theme axis revised (motion graduates, z-index excluded); alpha/lightness ambiguity marked resolved; .auto-color narrow contract pinned.

name: Implement async-state primitives (ADR 0005)
description: Implement , , .spinner, and button[aria-busy] styling per docs/adr/0005-async-state-primitives.md. in @layer base in src/lib/drop-in.css: - Reset native appearance: appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; border: none; background: var(--fg-1) - Track: border-radius: var(--br-pill); block-size: 0.5rem; inline-size: 100% (overrideable) - Value bar: ::-webkit-progress-bar { background: var(--fg-1) } ::-webkit-progress-value { background: var(--primary); border-radius: inherit } ::-moz-progress-bar { background: var(--primary) } - Indeterminate (no value attribute): animated stripe via background-image + @keyframes slide 1s linear infinite - --animation-reduced: slide 20s linear infinite (per ADR 0001 precedent) in @layer base: - Reset native appearance similarly - Default fill: --primary across the whole range - ::-webkit-meter-optimum-value, ::-webkit-meter-suboptimum-value, ::-webkit-meter-even-less-good-value all use --primary in neutral mode - .meter.signaling modifier in @layer components: optimum -> --green, suboptimum -> --yellow, even-less-good -> --red .spinner in @layer components: - - display: inline-block; inline-size: 1em; block-size: 1em; vertical-align: -0.125em - border: 2px solid color-mix(in oklab, currentColor 25%, transparent); border-block-start-color: currentColor; border-radius: 50% - animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite - @keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg) } } - --animation-reduced: none (stops entirely under prefers-reduced-motion per ADR 0001 + ADR 0005) - Size variants via --spinner-size custom property; consumers set font-size on parent for em-based scaling button[aria-busy="true"] in @layer components: - pointer-events: none - opacity: 0.7 - position: relative - Inset spinner via ::after pseudo-element (reuses .spinner styles or duplicates the rotation pattern) - Original button text remains visible (dimmed) so width doesn't change Tasks: 1. Add all four blocks to src/lib/drop-in.css in correct layers 2. Run npm run build to regenerate raw.js 3. Add demo markup to index.html: determinate progress, indeterminate progress, neutral meter, .meter.signaling meter at low/sub-optimum/optimum/high values, inline spinner in text, busy button (form submit pattern) 4. Update skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECIPES_COMPONENTS.md 5. Test reduced-motion: spinner should stop entirely, indeterminate progress should slow to ~20s cycle 6. Verify accessibility: , , role="status" + aria-label all announce correctly under VoiceOver/NVDA Skeleton is OUT OF SCOPE for this task per the ADR rationale.

name: Phase 3: Theme-agnostic templates and AI-legibility codification
description:
## Goal
Codify the theme-agnostic templates rule (ADR 0006), build the next round of template exemplars (auth, pricing, PDP, profile, search, inbox stretch), expand the AI-facing manifest with structured pattern metadata + gap notes, and tune Graffiti defaults toward neutral-and-clean so templates ship polished without inline aesthetic overrides.

## Why
Phase 2 (3ps0o37p) cleaned up several existing templates but did so without a codified rule about which overrides are permissible. The cleanup was uneven; the in-progress ai-chat retrofit stalled. This phase formalizes the rule, builds new templates against it, and re-audits Phase 2's completed retrofits.

## Primary goal
AI-legibility: templates become the canonical exemplar surface AI agents read via /templates with Accept: text/markdown. Gap discovery is secondary.

## Key rules (formalized in ADR 0006)
- Templates ship theme-agnostic. No inline aesthetic overrides (--primary, color-scheme, font-family, radius) at element level. Aesthetic personality comes from preset classes at :root (ADR 0003) or root-level token overrides, not from template internals.
- Inline-override classification:
  - Aesthetic: BANNED at element level. Blocks ship until Graffiti default is fixed.
  - Structural-recurring: gapNote on manifest highlight; ship; promote to component/modifier at >=3 instances.
  - Structural-one-off: allowed inline, no note.
- Graffiti's defaults target neutral-and-clean. Personality lives in presets, not defaults.
- Block-ship protocol: aesthetic gaps open a Dex task for the Graffiti fix; template task is blocked-by it; template lands clean (no workaround in source).

## Scope
- ADR 0006 + CONTEXT.md Template glossary entry + manifest schema migration (prose-highlights -> structured-highlights with gapNotes)
- Update SKILL.md to drop hardcoded template URL list, point at /templates, add Template Adaptation Rules section
- Build: auth (3 sub-routes), pricing (standalone), PDP, profile, search, inbox (stretch)
- Retrofit: docs-portal (no Phase 2 task); ai-chat fresh (Phase 2.10 never completed); re-audit landing/dashboard/blog/settings under ADR 0006
- Defaults audit: open container, populates as defaults issues surface during template builds

## Done when
- ADR 0006 written; CONTEXT.md updated; manifest schema migrated; SKILL.md aligned to /templates pointer
- 5+ new templates shipped at the quality bar (polished in defaults, no inline aesthetic overrides, structured-highlights with classes/tokens/anchor/gapNotes, multi-route where app inherently multi-screen)
- All existing templates pass ADR 0006 verification (grep clean, manifest-source drift check passes, manual visual review in light + dark)
- Verification: banned-overrides grep wired into pre-commit/CI; manifest-source drift check at render

## References
- ADR 0001 (reduced-motion contract), 0002 (cascade layer order), 0003 (aesthetic preset architecture; independent axis), 0004 (banner and empty state), 0005 (async-state primitives)
- Phase 2 epic 3ps0o37p (historical cleanup record)
- src/docs/agent/manifest.js, src/docs/agent/markdown.js, src/hooks.server.ts
- skills/graffiti-best-practices/SKILL.md

name: Phase 3.12: Build inbox template (stretch)
description:
## Goal
Stretch template - only build if the prior five (auth, pricing, PDP, profile, search) go cleanly and there's appetite. Three-pane (folders + list + reader) - classic split layout under load.

## Where
src/routes/templates/inbox/+page.svelte

## Pattern fidelity required
- Three-pane layout: folder/labels sidebar (left), message list (middle), message reader (right)
- On mobile: collapses to one pane at a time; nav between panes with back-button affordance
- Folder sidebar: nested .sidebar-nav with counts per folder
- Message list: dense rows with sender + subject + preview snippet + timestamp; unread = bold/dot indicator; threaded conversations grouped
- Reader: full message header (from/to/date/subject), action toolbar (reply/forward/archive), message body, attachment row
- Compose button (primary) prominent in sidebar or top
- Search bar across all panes (top-of-app affordance)
- Multi-select via checkboxes; bulk action toolbar appears when items selected
- Empty state when folder is empty (.empty)

## ADR 0006 rules
Same. Three-pane stresses .layout-* primitives heavily - watch for layout gaps.

## Manifest entry
Highlights:
- three-pane-layout (portable: false; inbox-specific composition; gapNote if no .layout-three-pane exists)
- folder-sidebar-with-counts (portable: false; high-context)
- message-list-row (portable: true)
- message-reader-pane (portable: false; specific)
- compose-button-affordance (portable: true)
- multi-select-bulk-actions (portable: true)
- empty-state (portable: true; uses .empty)

## Verification
- npm run check:templates passes
- Manifest-source drift check passes
- Manual visual review light + dark, mobile + desktop
- Pane collapse behavior on mobile is sensible (not horizontally scrolled disaster)

## Done when
- Template ships clean
- Manifest entry lands
- Graffiti fixes (if any) land first
- gapNotes recorded

## References
- ADR 0004 (.empty)
- ADR 0006
- Existing app-shell, sidebar-nav patterns (Phase 2.4)

- Contract language is specific and actionable, no vague guidance

name: Implement .rating component (radio-based stars)
description:
Add a pure HTML+CSS rating-stars component.

From docs/COMPONENT_COVERAGE.md, identified as one of 6 real remaining gaps.

Spec:
- Class in @layer components: .rating
- Markup: <fieldset class="rating"><legend>Rating</legend>
    <label aria-label="5 stars"><input type="radio" name="rating" value="5"></label>
    <label aria-label="4 stars"><input type="radio" name="rating" value="4"></label>
    …
  </fieldset>
- Display order: 5,4,3,2,1 in markup (so :checked ~ * sibling selector fills correctly when using reverse flex direction). Display via flex-direction: row-reverse so visual order is 1→5.
- Hide native radio: input { position: absolute; opacity: 0; pointer-events: none }
- Label = star: ::before content with star glyph (or SVG). Default color: var(--fg-2)
- Filled on hover OR checked: .rating label:hover, .rating label:hover ~ label, .rating input:checked + label, .rating input:checked + label ~ label { color: var(--yellow) }
- Read-only mode: .rating.readonly disables interaction (pointer-events: none on labels)
- Display-only variant: .rating.display can render filled stars from a CSS custom property --rating value (1-5) using nth-child gradients
- Composition with .row: works inside .row because .row trigger fires on <legend>/<fieldset>

Size: S
Pure HTML+CSS, no JS required. Form submission works via native radio name/value.

## Goal
Standalone script that scans src/routes/templates/** for inline aesthetic overrides banned by ADR 0006, fails fast on hits. Separate from the runtime manifest-source drift check (Phase 3.4) - that check runs at render; this one runs at commit/CI to catch the rule violation before code lands.

## Banned patterns to detect (regex within style= attributes inside templates):
- --primary[: ]

- color-scheme[: ]
- font-family[: ]

- Structural inline overrides: --gap, --layout-gap, --narrow-width, --span, --section-padding, --card-bg (when set to a token var, not a literal)

- scripts/check-template-overrides.js (or similar)
- package.json script entry: 'check:templates'
- Wire into pre-commit hook OR CI (decide during implementation; document choice in result)

## Output
- Zero hits: exit 0, no output
- Hits: exit 1, list file:line for each violation, with explanation citing ADR 0006

## Done when
- Script exists and runs in one command (npm run check:templates)
- Hooked into pre-commit OR CI
- Currently-clean templates pass; intentionally-dirty templates (ai-chat with forced color-scheme: dark) fail until they're retrofit in 3.14
- Documentation in package.json or scripts/README explaining intent

name: Phase 3.16: Graffiti defaults audit container (neutral-and-clean baseline)
description:
## Goal
Open container for tuning Graffiti's defaults toward the 'neutral-and-clean' philosophy declared in ADR 0006. Child tasks get created as template builds (3.7-3.14) surface specific defaults that need adjustment.

## Why this is a container
Per ADR 0006, Graffiti's defaults target neutral-and-clean - personality-free, restrained. Some current defaults likely carry more personality than the rule allows (e.g., default --primary blue saturation, default shadow scale, default radius scale, default --bubble-bg, default focus-ring color). Each specific tuning is its own task with its own scope and possibly its own ADR; this container groups them.

## How child tasks get created
When a template build (3.7-3.14) encounters an aesthetic override that traces back to a default being 'too opinionated' rather than missing, create a child task here:
- Title: 'Tune default <token> for neutral-and-clean baseline'
- Description: which template surfaced it, what the desired baseline is, what's blocked by it
- Block any in-progress template task on the child task (block-ship protocol)

## Candidate areas (discovered or anticipated)
- Default --primary saturation/lightness (current: vivid blue; neutral-and-clean baseline: more restrained)
- Default --bubble-bg and bubble surface defaults (current: not aligned with light-mode chat appearance; ai-chat compensates with overrides)
- Default shadow scale (--s-*) - are shadows too prominent for neutral?
- Default radius scale (--br-*) - too playful or right?
- Default focus-ring color and width
- Default --surface-bg distinction from --bg in light mode

## ADR follow-up
Each tuning that crosses the 'structural / surprising / hard-to-reverse' threshold gets its own ADR. Default value tweaks (e.g., changing the default --primary HSL by 10%) are below the bar; structural changes (e.g., introducing a new --surface-bg-2 token) are at the bar.

## This container 'completes' when
There's no closure - it stays open as a tracking surface. Mark it complete only when Phase 3 itself is closed and no defaults work remains queued.

## References
- ADR 0006 (neutral-and-clean philosophy)
- ADR 0003 (presets carry personality; defaults must NOT compete)

name: Phase 3.15: Re-audit completed Phase 2 retrofits under ADR 0006
description:
## Goal
Phase 2 retrofitted landing (69pllbj5), dashboard (bbduaimz), blog (e60rs1hr), and settings (h805g4hk) before ADR 0006 codified the theme-agnostic rule. The cleanup likely caught inline-style sprawl but may not have caught aesthetic-vs-structural classification with the rigor ADR 0006 demands. Re-audit all four.

## Approach
For EACH of the four templates (landing, dashboard, blog, settings):
1. Run npm run check:templates (3.6 grep) - record violations
2. Manually walk the source for inline style attributes; classify each per ADR 0006 (aesthetic / structural-recurring / structural-one-off)
3. For aesthetic violations: open child Dex tasks for Graffiti fixes; block this task; eliminate the overrides
4. For structural-recurring: gapNote in the manifest highlight; do not modify template
5. Migrate manifest entry to structured-highlights with anchors / classes / tokens / portable / gapNotes
6. Manual visual review light + dark, mobile + desktop

## Important
This is an audit, not a refactor. The Phase 2 retrofits left the templates in a 'mostly good' state. The audit's job is to find the residue: anywhere a Phase 2 fix accepted an inline override that ADR 0006 says is unacceptable. Don't gold-plate; fix what's wrong by the new rule.

## Sub-tracking
Consider creating child tasks per template (3.15.1 landing, 3.15.2 dashboard, etc.) if the workload is uneven; or handle all four in one pass and use --result to record per-template findings. Decide during execution.

## ADR 0006 rules
Same. Aesthetic = block ship; structural-recurring = gapNote; structural-one-off = inline allowed.

## Verification
- npm run check:templates passes for all four templates
- Manifest-source drift check passes for all four manifest entries
- Manual visual review light + dark for all four

## Done when
- All four templates pass grep + drift check + manual review
- Manifest entries migrated to structured-highlights
- Any child Graffiti fix tasks landed first
- gapNotes recorded for any residual structural-recurring patterns

## References
- ADR 0006
- Phase 2 task IDs for context: 69pllbj5 (landing), bbduaimz (dashboard), e60rs1hr (blog), h805g4hk (settings)
- Phase 3.3 (schema), 3.4 (drift check), 3.6 (grep)

name: Phase 1.4: Build anti-pattern and recovery playbook
description:
## Goal
Prevent common low-quality AI output patterns and provide deterministic recovery instructions.

## Work Required
- Document known Graffiti anti-patterns observed in templates and generated output:
  - Excessive inline style usage
  - Repeated margin resets and ad-hoc spacing
  - Hardcoded color overrides that bypass tokens
  - Semantically weak wrapper structures
  - Mixing multiple competing layout strategies in one section
- For each anti-pattern, provide:
  - Detection heuristics
  - Why it is problematic
  - Exact rewrite strategy using existing classes/tokens
- Define exception policy for temporary inline styles when no class exists

## Deliverables
- skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/ANTI_PATTERNS.md
- skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECOVERY_TRANSFORMS.md

## Done When
- Anti-pattern catalog covers top recurring failure modes from phase 1.1
- Recovery transforms are specific enough to execute mechanically
- Skill can point to exact rewrite guidance for each failure mode
result: Built the anti-pattern and deterministic recovery playbook deliverables: skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/ANTI_PATTERNS.md and skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECOVERY_TRANSFORMS.md. Cataloged recurring failure modes from phase 1.1 (inline style sprawl, ad-hoc spacing, hardcoded colors, semantic wrapper debt, competing layouts, unknown classes, 100vh shell misuse, oversized token dumps, JS-first interaction patterns), added explicit detection heuristics and rationale, and provided mechanical before/after rewrite transforms with ordered execution + verification checks. Included a strict temporary inline exception policy for cases where no class variant exists.

name: Phase 3.11: Build search/results-with-filters template
description:
## Goal

name: Aesthetic presets: CSS authoring + distribution pipeline
description:
Author the v1 preset class files, wire the opt-in import path, and extend the build pipeline.

Per ADR-0003 (docs/adr/0003-aesthetic-preset-architecture.md), aesthetic presets are first-class CSS classes shipped opt-in (separate from drop-in.css). Consumers import `@drop-in/graffiti/themes/<preset>` for one or `@drop-in/graffiti/themes` for all. The build pipeline must emit raw JS modules for each preset file alongside CSS.

v1 catalog (ADR-0003): theme-editorial, theme-brutalist, theme-soft-consumer, theme-neon-arcade, theme-paper.

Authoring discipline: token-dominant (~95% variable overrides). Selector rules reserved for typographic decoration only — ::first-letter, ::selection, text-transform, letter-spacing. Never structural; never component-class selectors. All selector rules live inside `@layer themes { ... }` (declared in Epic 1).

name: Fix viewport-based layout issues
description:
Beads description:
Several Graffiti components use viewport-based units (vw, vh) which cause issues when nested inside constrained containers like sidebar layouts.

**Problematic components:**
- .header - uses viewport-based sizing
- .full-bleed - breaks out to viewport width
- .swipe.full-bleed - same issue

**The problem:**
When these components are used inside a sidebar layout or any constrained container, they overflow or push the container because they reference the viewport, not their container.

**Potential solutions to explore:**
1. Container queries - make layouts auto-set container-type on main content areas
2. Documentation changes - show these components in appropriate contexts, warn about viewport limitations
3. Modifiers - add .contained variants that use container-relative units
4. Rethink the components - maybe full-bleed shouldn't use vw at all

**Related:**
- Fluid typography system also uses vw but has .fc class for container-based sizing
- May need to audit all vw/vh usage in drop-in.css

This epic should result in either:
- Fixed components that work in nested contexts
- Clear documentation about when/where to use these components
- Or both

name: Phase 3.5: Update SKILL.md - /templates pointer + Template Adaptation Rules
description:
## Goal
Three concrete edits to skills/graffiti-best-practices/SKILL.md:

## Edit 1: Drop hardcoded template URL list (SKILL.md:73)
Replace the line that enumerates six template URLs with a single pointer to https://graffiti-ui.com/templates fetched with Accept: text/markdown. The index page (via renderTemplatesIndexMarkdown) already enumerates every template from manifest.js, so new templates auto-surface without a skill edit.

Concrete diff:
- Remove: '2. https://graffiti-ui.com/templates and template pages such as ...landing...dashboard...blog...settings...ai-chat...docs-portal'
- Replace with: '2. https://graffiti-ui.com/templates (Accept: text/markdown) - returns the current template index with summaries, classes, tokens, anchors, and gapNotes per pattern. Fetch this index first to see all templates; then fetch the specific template MD as needed.'

## Edit 2: Add 'Template Adaptation Rules' section
Insert after 'Variable System Rules' and before 'Required Workflow'. Three rules (cite ADR 0006):

> ### Template Adaptation Rules
> 
> 1. **Templates are theme-agnostic exemplars.** They use Graffiti's default appearance. When a consumer wants a distinctive look ('dark', 'editorial', 'neon'), apply a theme class at :root (when presets exist) or override tokens at :root. NEVER propagate inline aesthetic overrides (--primary, color-scheme, font-family, radius) from a template baseline into the consumer's page. (Source: ADR 0006.)
> 2. **Surface gapNotes when present.** If the template's MD includes 'Known limitations' for a pattern (from highlight.gapNotes), the skill output names this limitation and offers the closest current approach. Don't pretend the gap doesn't exist.
> 3. **Preserve canonical composition.** Existing rule.

## Edit 3: Add ADR 0006 to Graffiti Identity references
At SKILL.md ~line 75, add ADR docs path under source-of-truth ordering, OR add explicit citation in the new Template Adaptation Rules section. Either works.

## Files
- skills/graffiti-best-practices/SKILL.md (3 edits)

name: Grill: Layout primitive gaps
description:
Stress-test what's missing or broken in Graffiti's layout-* primitives.

Open threads:
- .layout-sidebar.fill mobile collapse bug (LESSONS.md): container stays at height:100dvh while the grid collapses to 1fr, sidebar takes its natural ~400-500px, main pane trapped at ~156px. Fix options: reset height:auto at mobile, opt-in collapse modifier, drawer-only on mobile, something else?
- Two mobile sidebar flavors exist (drawer popover vs hidden aside). Is the contract clear enough? Should there be a third (bottom-sheet on mobile)?
- Missing layout primitives to consider: master-detail (list+detail with deep linking), split-pane (resizable), centered-with-rails, full-bleed-within-readable, sticky-rail. Which of these are real, vs. expressible by composition?
- Responsive contract: layouts collapse at fixed breakpoints (768px, 500px). Should consumers be able to override the collapse threshold per instance via a custom prop, or is that a footgun?
- Layout vs. utility classification: .flex/.split/.cluster/.grid sit in utilities but feel layout-shaped. CONTEXT.md already flags this. Does the cascade-order ADR fully resolve it, or do we need to reclassify some?

Resume by running: /grill-with-docs on Graffiti layout primitive gaps
result:
Resolved all 5 threads.

ADRs written:
- ADR-0009: layout vs utility classification rule (responsive/child-contract = layout; atomic = utility). .split moves to layouts, .cluster moves to utilities.
- ADR-0010: repurpose .layout-holy-grail as centered-content-with-rails primitive with .rail-start/.rail-end children. .layout-three-col stays as equal-columns.

Resolved without ADRs:
- T1 mobile sidebar collapse bug: stale, code already handles it
- T2 mobile sidebar flavors: drawer/hidden contract clear; bottom-sheet folds into .drawer with .left/.right/.top/.bottom direction modifiers
- T4 breakpoint overridability: hold the line, fixed thresholds are structural contract
- T3 other candidates: master-detail (sidebar+route), split-pane (JS), full-bleed (already shipped), sticky-rail (already covered)

CONTEXT.md: Layout primitive and Utility entries rewritten with classification rule; flagged ambiguity resolved; .layout-three-col vs .layout-holy-grail intent distinction added.

name: Phase 3.14: Retrofit ai-chat template under ADR 0006 (fresh)
description:
## Goal
Fresh retrofit. Supersedes Phase 2.10 (wb3hpt6c, closed). ai-chat is the most visibly broken template under ADR 0006 - has forced color-scheme: dark, --primary: #24bf73 at root, and per-bubble color overrides via --bubble-bg/--bubble-border on each .self bubble.

## Where
src/routes/templates/ai-chat/+page.svelte
src/docs/agent/manifest.js (update ai-chat entry to structured highlights)

## Aesthetic overrides to eliminate (these BLOCK ship)
- Line 3: color-scheme: dark; background: var(--bg); color: var(--fg); --primary: #24bf73
- Per-bubble: style='--bubble-bg: var(--green-1); --bubble-border: var(--green-5);' on each .self bubble

## Approach
The 'chat vibe' (dark, green-accented) is NOT the template's job under ADR 0006. The retrofitted template should:
- Render polished in Graffiti defaults (light, neutral accent)
- Distinguish self vs assistant bubbles structurally - .bubble.self modifier vs .bubble; if .bubble.self doesn't exist as a modifier, OPEN A DEX TASK for the new modifier and block this task on it
- Have NO color-scheme declaration in source - consumer's system or preset decides

## Surface dark/green vibe how?
Consumers wanting a 'dark tech-green' chat apply a theme class at :root once aesthetic presets ship (ADR 0003). For now, document in the template's manifest highlight 'how to re-skin' as a tokens-at-root example (NOT inline-style example).

## ADR 0006 rules
Aesthetic overrides BANNED at element level. .bubble.self modifier is structural-recurring (or one-off if only ai-chat needs it; if other templates need a 'self' alignment too, promote).

## Manifest entry
Highlights:
- chat-shell-with-sidebar (portable: false; ai-chat-specific layout)
- conversation-list (portable: false; high-context)
- chat-thread (portable: true)
- message-bubble-pair (portable: true; .bubble + .bubble.self modifier)
- chat-composer (portable: true; uses .input-group)
- agent-identity-header (portable: false; high-context)

## Verification
- npm run check:templates passes for ai-chat
- Manifest-source drift check passes
- Manual visual review light + dark
- ai-chat is no longer hidden in templates index (the original 2.10 acceptance)

## Done when
- Zero aesthetic inline overrides in source
- Structural-modifier-based bubble differentiation
- Manifest entry is structured-highlights
- All verification passes
- Any child Dex tasks for Graffiti changes landed first

## References
- ADR 0006
- Original Phase 2.10 wb3hpt6c (closed as superseded - read its description for context on the structural cleanup that was attempted)

name: Docs Single Source of Truth Migration
description:
## Goal
Make the .md topic files in src/docs/content/topics/ the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for both:
1. Human-facing docs site (rendered as HTML with live demos)
2. Agent/LLM markdown (served via content negotiation with Accept: text/markdown)

## Current State
- 39 topic .md files exist in src/docs/content/topics/
- These ONLY power agent markdown via content negotiation on /base, /elements, /utilities, /ui-blocks
- Human docs are SEPARATE hardcoded Svelte files in src/routes/(docs)/*.svelte
- This means TWO sources of truth that can drift

## Architecture Overview
- Topics are the unit of content (one .md file per topic)
- Each topic has: id, title, route, order, summary, when_to_use, classes, demos, tags, markdown body
- Demos live in src/docs/demos/*.svelte - pure HTML, no JS behavior
- Demo registry maps demo names to components + raw code
- TopicSection component renders a topic with its demos
- Route pages dynamically render all topics for that route
- Sidebar auto-generates sub-nav from topic titles

## Key Files
- src/docs/content/topics/*.md - 39 topic files (source of truth)
- src/docs/content/schema.js - validates topic frontmatter
- src/docs/content/runtime.js - loads topics at runtime
- src/docs/demos/*.svelte - 40 demo components
- src/routes/(docs)/*.svelte - current hardcoded pages (to be replaced)
- history/docs-agent-inventory-2026-03-17.md - baseline inventory

## Demo Rules
- Demos can use {#each} over static arrays for templating
- NO $state, NO onclick, NO $effect, NO JS behavior
- Pure HTML output
- Each demo is a .svelte file in src/docs/demos/

## Success Criteria
- All docs content lives in .md topic files
- Human docs render correctly with live demos + code display
- Agent markdown unchanged (test with curl -H 'Accept: text/markdown')
- Adding new content = edit one .md file + optionally add demo
- Sidebar auto-generates from topics
- Visual parity with current docs site
result: All 12 migration tasks are complete. Human docs routes (/base, /utilities, /elements, /ui-blocks) now render from topic markdown as a single source of truth, sidebar sub-navigation is generated from route topics, and route intros are markdown-backed. name: Phase 3.7: Build auth template (login + register + forgot-password)
description:
## Goal
First new template under ADR 0006. Auth is the canonical stress test of .row (ADR 0003 row-as-form-field-stack) and the form-side defaults that will benefit four subsequent templates (pricing, PDP profile, search).

## Where
src/routes/templates/auth/
  login/+page.svelte
  register/+page.svelte
  forgot-password/+page.svelte
(plus parent +layout.svelte if shared shell makes sense - decide during build)

## Pattern fidelity required (the 'VERY good' bar)
- Centered card-on-page composition; sparse, focused
- Form fields use .row pattern (label, small hint, input, output for error) per ADR 0003
- Social-auth row (e.g., 3 .button.minimal buttons in .cluster) with SVG icons
- 'or continue with' divider (horizontal rule with centered text - if this needs a new class, flag as structural-recurring gapNote)
- Trust copy under primary CTA ('No credit card required', 'Terms apply')
- Inline validation styling (:user-invalid revealed via .row's <output>)
- register: password strength meter (use <meter class='meter signaling'> per ADR 0005)
- forgot-password: confirmation state after submit + resend link
- Real keyboard navigation; focus order matches visual order
- Light + dark both polished

## ADR 0006 rules
- NO inline aesthetic overrides (--primary, color-scheme, font-family, radius). If a polish problem can only be solved with one, OPEN A DEX TASK for the Graffiti fix, mark this auth task blocked-by it, then return.
- Structural-recurring inline overrides: gapNote on the manifest highlight; continue.
- Structural-one-off inline overrides: allowed; no note.

## Manifest entry (after Phase 3.3 schema)
Add a templatePages entry for /templates/auth (and per-sub-route entries if applicable):
- slug, routePath, title, summary, bestFor
- highlights array with structured objects per pattern (form-stack, social-row, divider, trust-copy, password-strength, confirmation state)
- Each highlight: name, classes, tokens, anchor (with matching <section id> in source), portable (true for divider/trust-copy/social-row; false for the form composition specific to auth)
- gapNotes when relevant

## Verification (must all pass before complete)
- npm run check:templates passes (no banned overrides)
- Manifest-source drift check passes (3.4)
- Manual visual review in light + dark, mobile + desktop
- Real form submit behavior (action attribute valid, no JS gymnastics)

## Done when
- 3 sub-routes ship clean
- Manifest entries land with structured highlights
- Any Graffiti fixes triggered (blocked-by chain) are landed first
- Any gapNotes recorded for future promotion review

## References
- ADR 0003 (row as form-field stack)
- ADR 0005 (async-state primitives - for password strength meter)
- ADR 0006 (theme-agnostic)
- Quality bar criteria from Phase 3 epic description

name: Phase 2.3: Add component variants for recurring template styling patterns
description:
## Goal
Reduce one-off component styling by adding reusable variants where templates currently rely on inline overrides.

## Work Required
- Identify repeated inline component customizations (tag colors, featured states, card emphasis, compact controls, etc.)
- Add class-based variants or tokenized modifiers for these patterns
- Preserve backwards compatibility for existing class usage where possible
- Document variant usage with concise examples in llms docs

## Files
- src/lib/drop-in.css
- static/llms/tags.txt
- static/llms/card.txt
- static/llms/buttons.txt
- Any other relevant component docs

## Done When
- Common component-level inline overrides are replaced by documented variants
- Variants are theme-safe and accessible
- Template refactor tasks can consume these variants directly
result: Completed via prior Phase 2.3 implementation: added reusable semantic component variants (status tags, linked/featured cards, and form option patterns), updated demos/docs, and published in v4.20.0 to replace recurring inline template styling patterns with class-first variants.

name: ADR-0013: extend contract tests for public surface + strict reduced-motion rule
description: Add to tests/contracts/ suite: (1) cascade layer order assertion (verify @layer base, themes, components, utilities, layouts; is intact in bundled CSS). (2) Layer-assignment assertions for ADR-0009 (.split in layouts, .cluster in utilities). (3) New-token presence assertions per ADR-0006 (.theme-scope re-derivation block), ADR-0007 (--{base}-opaque-1..9), ADR-0008 (--d-* and --z-*). (4) Strict --animation-reduced rule: any CSS rule containing animation: must also declare --animation-reduced (none or a variant). Implement after the corresponding ADRs land. See docs/adr/0013-contract-test-enforcement.md.

name: Phase 2.12: Add regression guardrails for template quality
description:
## Goal
Prevent reintroduction of template anti-patterns by adding lightweight, repeatable quality checks.

## Work Required
- Add a script/check that reports inline style counts per template and flags threshold regressions
- Add a check for unknown/non-existent class usage in templates
- Add optional checks for required semantic landmarks in full-page templates
- Document how to run checks locally and how to interpret failures

## Files
- scripts/ or bin/ quality-check script(s)
- package.json scripts if needed
- Documentation for quality checks

## Done When
- Checks can be run locally in one command
- Output is clear enough for AI and humans to act on
- Baseline thresholds are captured and enforceable
result: Superseded by Phase 3 verification work: grep for banned aesthetic overrides per ADR 0006 (zero-tolerance at element level, not threshold-count), and manifest-source drift check at render time. The original inline-style-count-threshold framing was softer than ADR 0006 requires.

name: Base route visual parity fixes
description:
## Goal
Address regressions discovered after migrating /base to dynamic topic rendering.

## Issues observed
- Typography topic markdown rendered large nested headings and long code-heavy prose, creating noisy layout.
- Legacy base sections expected by users (Colors, Dividers) were missing as separate topics.
- Legacy deep-link target #forms was not present due topic id being form-defaults.

## Planned changes
- Simplify base-typography.md and base-variables.md body content to concise guidance.
- Add base-colors.md topic (ColorScales/ThemingVariables demos).
- Add base-dividers.md topic (Dividers demo).
- Update base-form-defaults.md title to Forms and provide legacy #forms anchor in markdown body.

## Verification
- npm run check:docs-content
- npm run build
- agent-browser visual/runtime check on /base (section headings, anchors, demo loading).
result: Implemented base route parity fixes by simplifying base typography/variables markdown bodies, adding new base colors and dividers topics with existing demos, and adding a legacy #forms anchor in the forms topic. Verified with npm run check:docs-content, npm run build, and agent-browser on /base (headings Typography/CSS Variables/Colors/Dividers/Forms present, anchors #typography/#variables/#colors/#dividers/#forms resolve, no demo load errors, no runtime overlay).

name: Implement .row as form-field stack (ADR 0003)
description:
Implement the decisions captured in docs/adr/0003-row-as-form-field-stack.md.

Tasks:
1. Move .row from @layer utilities (line ~1263) to @layer components in src/lib/drop-in.css
2. Drop the :is(form, fieldset) ancestor gate; replace with :has() trigger on direct form children: .row:has(> :is(label, legend, fieldset, input, select, textarea))
3. Add hint slot rule: <small> direct child styling within the trigger
4. Add error slot rule: > output { display: none } by default; .row:has(:user-invalid) > output { display: block } with --error color
5. Add required asterisk: .row:has(> :required) > :is(label, legend)::after { content: '*'; color: var(--error, currentColor); }
6. Add option-group support: .row that is a <fieldset> or contains <fieldset>; treat <legend> as label
7. Add .row.horizontal modifier: two-column grid (label-and-hint left, input-and-output right), use grid-template-areas
8. Add .row.compact modifier: tightened --row-gap and reduced font-size for hint/error
9. Add invalid label tint: .row:has(:user-invalid) > :is(label, legend) { color: var(--error) }
10. Add disabled fade: .row:has(:disabled) > :is(label, legend, small) { color: var(--fg-3); opacity: 0.6 }
11. Update existing .row callsites in src/lib/drop-in.css (if any rely on the old form-gated behavior)
12. Run npm run build to regenerate raw.js
13. Update skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/RECIPES_COMPONENTS.md with new .row pattern
14. Add tests/contracts: vitest assertion that .row produces field-stack grid when children are form elements; that <output> is hidden by default
15. Verify in index.html demo page

Open implementation nuance from grilling:
- Should .row go ON the <fieldset> directly (recommended) or wrap it in an outer div? ADR 0003 picks 'on the fieldset' but this is testable.
- For horizontal modifier, exact column ratio: minmax(8rem, 1fr) / 2fr is a reasonable default, expose via --row-label-size custom property.
- For output reveal, decide whether to use display: none -> block, or visibility, or content-visibility. Display is simplest.

name: Add worked aesthetic example to EXAMPLES.md
description: Edit skills/graffiti-best-practices/references/EXAMPLES.md. Add a prompt/response pair: user asks for a brutalist landing page in Graffiti. Response shows class=theme-brutalist applied at html, then a standard landing template structure with no additional inline color/typography choices. Demonstrates preset-first then trust-the-system.
